behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is rule logic inside the scoring model: compare amount vs merchant-category threshold and compute score. No behavior text requires module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or AI/ML judgment. Rejected Integration because the behavior is scoped to model logic itself ("scoring model assigns the correct risk score"), not cross-module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function with in-memory config containing per-category thresholds and a transaction just above threshold; assert the exact numeric score increment/assignment for that category.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment over behavioral context (pattern recognition beyond explicit thresholds). A stubbed model would hide failures in actual fraud prediction quality. Rejected Workflow because behavior describes one model decision step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on labeled transaction sequences that encode cross-geo rapid small-purchase fraud patterns with no scoring-rule breach; assert fraud probability/label crosses the fraud decision threshold at acceptable detection quality.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is temporal counting logic (filter by user and window, then count). Behavior text does not require real DB, filesystem events, subprocesses, or network. Rejected Integration because no explicit module boundary is required by the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke velocity-check logic with an in-memory transaction list spanning inside/outside window boundaries for multiple users and a fixed reference time; assert per-user counts and boundary inclusivity rules.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff/orchestration boundary: combining outputs from two model components into review-hold decision. No need for real OS/network/subprocess. Not Agentic because this behavior fixes model outputs as inputs ("flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model") and verifies boolean gate semantics only, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring-model adapter, risk-model adapter, and decision service in-process; feed a case where scoring=false and risk=true; assert transaction status is held-for-review. Add complementary cases to confirm OR semantics.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: Behavior validates contract composition across components: merged output must carry fields from both models and attribution of which flagged. No real infrastructure required, and no dependence on real AI/ML quality (only propagation of produced values). Rejected Agentic because assertion is about output schema/content pass-through, not whether model probabilities are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Execute aggregation path with deterministic model outputs (e.g., score=72, fraud_probability=0.91, flag sources); assert response payload contains both values and explicit per-model flag attribution for reviewer context.
